description.text:
### Checklist

- [x] Zettlr does not possess this feature (if it does, but the feature doesn't work out for you, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [ ] Zettlr's current features cannot be used to approximate this behaviour (otherwise, propose an enhancement instead.)
- [ ] I have searched the documentation to make sure I did not overlook it.
- [ ] I have searched both open and closed issues to see if someone has already proposed this.

### Feature Proposal

Most browsers have a shortcut, `Ctrl-Shift-t` to reopen the last-closed tab (invoking the shortcut multiple times reopens additional recently closed tabs in reverse chronological order). I'd love to see this feature in Zettlr, as I will not infrequently close a tab by accident and would rather not have to locate it again in the file manager to re-open it.

### Example Workflow

See above.

### Caveats

None.

### Do you wish to attempt implementing this yourself?

No

### Platforms

- [x] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [x] Linux

### Additional Information

Thank you, as always!

summary: Full-Text Search Directory Filter Does Not Scroll

description.text:
### Description

4.0.0-beta and 4.0.0-beta2

When executing a full-text search, the second input to restrict the search to a specific directory does not allow scrolling by mouse wheel or scroll bar.   Arrow keys do seem to send the selection down below the visible options but the user interface does not scroll down to track the selection.   This impedes search functionality for users with many directories or workspaces who would like to restrict a search to a specific directory.

### Reproducing

1.  Have many workspaces with a few directories each or a single workspace with many directories.
2.  Initiate a full-text search in the left sidebar.
3.  In the second input field, use the drop-down to open the list of directories.
4.  Attempt to scroll said list.

### Zettlr version

4.0.0-beta2

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Win11

### Additional Information

Zettlr is being used in the portable fashion.

summary: File list doesn't update in WebDAV working directories

description.text:
### Description

Some of my working directories are saved in places I map as network drives via WebDAV into my local system.

In each one of them (and only in them) the list of files isn't updated: If I add a new file, it isn't shown in the list, if I change the name of an existing file in the list, the new file name doesn't show up. If I create a new file in Zettlr, I can edit it and it is shown as a tab on top of the editor, but it doesn't show up in the list and there's no context menu on right click for its tab. If I add a file to the directory directly (not via Zettlr, create file) it doesn't show up in the list. Changes in file names work on a file-system level, but aren't displayed. Moving files into other directories works on a file-system level too, but only into different WebDAV directories, not in "true local" directories (and vice versa). Exporting files that aren't shown in the file list doesn't work either – I see the export dialogue, but nothing happens when I click the export button.

I I close Zettlr and start it again, the file list is updated and everything works as it should with the files present at start. The problems occur consistently, so I don't think that it's just because the WebDAV directories are slower to access than true local directories.

### Reproducing

Map a network drive via WebDAV in Windows 11, add a directory on this network drive as working directory in Zettlr, create a new file or rename an existing one.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11 Enterprise, 24H2

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Switching between ToC, references, and related files panels takes considerable time

description.text:
### Description

The panels to the right have become quite slow in the new Beta 4. Switching between them takes some time, and even sometimes hangs the whole app and I get "app not responding" message in Windows.

### Reproducing

- Open a note.
- Click on the different panes in the right-side panel, switching between ToC, references, and related files.
- Sometimes, the switching can take more than 10-12 seconds while the application becomes non-responsive.

### Zettlr version

Version 4 Beta 2 latest nightly

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

It might be that this is a caching issue since the operation becomes faster after the first time that this delay happens.

### Description

Zettlr supports workspace-based projects, which is an absolutely great feature for writing projects. However, to create a project one has to create a workspace first because the project settings are connected to a workspace and not to a note. I think Zettlr would become much more flexible as a project tool if projects were note-based instead of workspace-based. Of course this is based on my own way of working Zettlr. But I think a note-based approach would cover all that a workspace-based approach offers and more.

### Proposed Changes

This is the enhancement I propose: 

- Right-click on any note in the browser. The enhancement would provide an "Enable project" option for that note.
- Enable note as a project. This node then becomes the first file in the newly created project.
- From now on, you can do the usual stuff you can do with a project in Zettlr, such as adding assigning an export template, adding and ordering other notes to the project, and any future setting already proposed by many, e.g., setting the default language, folder to export to etc.

From my way of using Zettlr, this approach would make working with project more flexible and open for new functionalities that may not be possible with the current solution:

- We can stop thinking in folders and continue thinking in flat note collections with tags. A more streamlined approach in my opinion.
-  All the pros of tag-based note management will be preserved also for projects for instance,
- A note can be added to multiple projects.
- We can add notes to projects based on tags, meaning that if I create a new note with a tag then it will be automatically included in the project. I can for instance assign a tag to a book project. (This can also be an annoyance, so maybe it should come with an option to exclude individual notes!).
- We can extract "snapshots" of different parts of a zettelkasten at any moment by creating say a PDF of all notes tagged with a specific keyword (as C Wright Mills would do!) without first moving files around.
- We can have nested projects, for instance, part one of a book can be a project itself.
- etc.

### Caveats

- The main caveat is that we need to move project creation option from workspace to individual notes. Probably many Zettlr users, like myself, have some projects already that needs to be converted. Remember that the enhancement I propose does not prevent one from still creating one workspace for each project, as we do now. In that case, one will choose a note within that workspace as the project file. Maybe we can even have a project option to include all files in the folder? (can be a disaster if used accidentally in a large zettelkasten folder!)
- Another caveat I see is that note that you want to add to your newly created project might already be a project itself. In that case, the project status of that note needs to be ignored in the context of this new project, or we can allow for nested projects.
- A third caveat is that I think this can be a time-consuming enhancement!

### Do you Wish to Attempt Implementing this Yourself?

No

### Zettlr Version

Nightly

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

Zettlr provides the possibility to write [[ and see a pop-up list of notes to link to. This pop-up windows fails to show up sometime, more often since the beta 4 version.

Inside a note:

summary: Focus (cursor) is lost after undoing a table in "Rendering: Preview" mode

description.text:
### Description

Undoing a table in "Rendering: Preview" mode loses the cursor. Undoing still works, but redoing doesn't if the document isn't clicked.



### Reproducing

1) create a new md
2) Rendering: Preview mode on
3) write a line of lorem ipsum
4) create a table
5) press undo until the document is empty
6) cursor disappears after undoing the table
7) redo doesn't work without clicking the screen


### Zettlr version

v4.0.0-beta.2

summary: [Fix] Update rendering mode immediately on statusbar change

description.text:
<!--
  Thank you for opening up this pull request! Please read this comment carefully
  and make sure to fill in as much info as possible below as well.

  First, the obligatory checklist. You must make sure to follow this list as
  much as possible to make merging your PR as easy as possible:

  1. I documented all behaviour within the code as far as I could.
  2. This PR targets the develop branch and *not* the master branch.
  3. I have tested my changes extensively and paid extra attention to potential
     cross-platform issues (e./g. Cmd/Ctrl/Super key bindings)
  4. I ran the `yarn lint` and `yarn test` commands successfully
  5. I have added an entry to the CHANGELOG.md
  6. I matched my code-style to the repository as far as possible
  7. I agree that my code will be published under the GNUP GPL v3 license
  8. In case I created new files, I added a header to them (see the existing
     files for examples)
  9. Before opening this PR, I ran `git pull` a last time to prevent any merge
     issues

  If you don't know how to fulfill some of the above points, feel free to open
  your PR nevertheless and mention those points where you are unsure. The more
  you can fulfill, the faster we can merge your PR, otherwise it will just take
  longer.
 -->

## Description
<!-- Below, please describe what the PR does in one or two short sentences. -->
This PR reconfigures the renderer handling so that changing the rendering mode from the statusbar immediately updates the editor.

Closes #6007

## Changes
<!-- What changes did you make? Please explicitly state any breaking API changes so that nobody is confused why other components suddenly stop working -->
Functionally, the `renderingModeToggle` was updated to use `dispatch` events to update the config rather than `window.config.set`, and an empty dispatch was added so that the changed mode is applied without further interaction from the user.

Additionally, the code was refactored for maintainability, notably moving the configuration of renderers to its own function.

## Additional information
<!-- If there is anything else that might be of interest, please provide it here -->

<!-- Please provide any testing system -->
Tested on: <!-- OS including version --> macOS 26

summary: Tag manager rename tag function broken

description.text:
### Description

The tag manager has an option to rename tag. While this seems to work, it is only within the tag manager that the change is applied. 
Actually the tags are no changed in the files. 
So once you do this, it is impossible to locate the new or old tags. the new  because it doesnt actually exist, the old because you didnt have the foresight to write it down and trusted a button and text entry for Rename tag would actually rename tag. 
After deleting every conceivable json file and cache i could, nothing resolved the issue. i have had to delete my entire config folder to reset the tags to their originals.
Either fix this bug so that it works or remove the option, so people like me dont spend 2 hours trying to find imaginery tags and then have to reset their system

### Reproducing

go to the tag manager - use the rename function built in (make it something unique so that it isnt shared with an existing tag) - do a search for the new renamed tag. you can search using zettlr, obsidian, vs code or terminal. you wont find it becuase it doesnt exist. the tags have not actually been renamed but tag manager will never show the real tag again, just the false renamed tag Version 3.6.0 (3.6.0) mac

### Zettlr version

Version 3.6.0 (3.6.0) mac

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

macos 15.7.1 (24G231)

_No response_
